components.name: sodium DL-lactate
components.source: cited:Paulick 2009 (LM medium; restated in Kreienbaum et al. 2020, Rick et al. 2022)
components.evidence: LM medium (10 mM HEPES, pH 7.5; 100 mM NaCl; 0.02% yeast extract; 0.01% peptone; 15 mM lactate) [Kreienbaum et al. 2020, PMC7530303]; 'lactate medium (LM): 10 mM HEPES, pH 7.5, 200 mM NaCl, 0.02% (w/v) yeast extract, 0.01% (w/v) peptone, 15 mM lactate' [Rick et al. 2022, PMC9061725]

components.name: sodium fumarate
components.evidence: All evolution and plasmid persistence assays were carried out in lactate mineral medium (Paulick et al. 2009) supplemented with 0.015 M sodium fumarate (LMF). Sodium fumarate was added as an alternative electron acceptor because oxygen can be limiting in our system.

components.name: HEPES (sodium HEPES)
components.source: cited:Paulick 2009 (LM medium; restated in Kreienbaum et al. 2020, Rick et al. 2022)
components.evidence: LM medium (10 mM HEPES, pH 7.5; 100 mM NaCl; 0.02% yeast extract; 0.01% peptone; 15 mM lactate) [Kreienbaum et al. 2020, PMC7530303]

components.name: sodium chloride (NaCl)
components.source: cited:Paulick 2009 (LM medium; restated in Kreienbaum et al. 2020)
components.evidence: LM medium (10 mM HEPES, pH 7.5; 100 mM NaCl; 0.02% yeast extract; 0.01% peptone; 15 mM lactate) [Kreienbaum et al. 2020, PMC7530303]. NOTE: a 200 mM NaCl variant is used in Rick et al. 2022; Paulick 2009 (Wiley, paywalled) could not be read directly.

components.name: yeast extract
components.role: undefined complex ingredient (supplies N source, phosphate, sulfate, K, Mg, Ca, trace metals Fe/Zn/Mn/Co/Cu, and vitamins)
components.source: cited:Paulick 2009 (LM medium; restated in Kreienbaum et al. 2020, Rick et al. 2022)
components.evidence: LM medium (10 mM HEPES, pH 7.5; 100 mM NaCl; 0.02% yeast extract; 0.01% peptone; 15 mM lactate) [Kreienbaum et al. 2020, PMC7530303]

components.name: peptone
components.source: cited:Paulick 2009 (LM medium; restated in Kreienbaum et al. 2020, Rick et al. 2022)
components.evidence: LM medium (10 mM HEPES, pH 7.5; 100 mM NaCl; 0.02% yeast extract; 0.01% peptone; 15 mM lactate) [Kreienbaum et al. 2020, PMC7530303]

components.name: gentamicin
components.role: antibiotic selection (evolution phase only; omitted in persistence assays)
components.evidence: Experimental evolution was performed in LMF supplemented with 10 mg/l of Gm selecting for the retention of the plasmid pBP136Gm, and the plasmid persistence assays were done in the same medium without Gm.

media.base_medium: LM medium (lactate medium) of the Thormann lab, cited by Stalder et al. as Paulick et al. 2009: 10 mM HEPES (pH 7.4-7.5), 100 mM NaCl (200 mM in some variants), 0.02% (w/v) yeast extract, 0.01% (w/v) peptone, 15 mM lactate.

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as 'lactate mineral medium (Paulick et al. 2009)' = Paulick A, Koerdt A, Lassak J, Huntley S, Wilms I, Narberhaus F, Thormann KM. 2009. Two different stator systems drive a single polar flagellum in Shewanella oneidensis MR-1. Mol Microbiol 71(4):836. doi:10.1111/j.1365-2958.2008.06570.x
sources_chased.how: WebFetch of Wiley full text/PDF returned HTTP 403 (paywalled); recipe reconstructed from concordant restatements in Thormann-lab papers that cite Paulick 2009 for LM medium

sources_chased.what: LM medium verbatim recipe + Paulick attribution
sources_chased.how: WebFetch Kreienbaum et al. 2020 (PMC7530303), Rick et al. 2022 (PMC9061725), Binnenkade et al. 2018 (PMC6085458), Godeke et al. 2011 (PMC3105746) - all Thormann-lab papers citing 'LM medium (Paulick et al., 2009)'
added: confirmed LM medium = HEPES + NaCl + 0.02% yeast extract + 0.01% peptone + 15 mM lactate
confirmed Paulick 2009 is the defining reference

missing_before: HEPES buffer (10 mM) - the actual buffer (medium is NOT phosphate-buffered)
yeast extract (0.02% w/v) - undefined complex ingredient supplying N, P, S, K, Mg, Ca, trace metals and vitamins
media.notes: Despite being called 'lactate mineral medium' by Stalder et al., the Paulick 2009 'LM medium' is a HEPES-buffered lactate medium containing yeast extract + peptone as complex ingredients, NOT a fully defined mineral medium with a salts/trace-element stock. Nitrogen, phosphate, sulfate, K, Mg, Ca and all trace metals (Fe, Zn, Mn, Co, Cu) and vitamins are supplied by the 0.02% yeast extract + 0.01% peptone (which the DB decomposes separately), so the ion exchanges in the original component list are justified by these complex ingredients rather than by defined salts. LMF = LM medium + 0.015 M (15 mM) sodium fumarate as an alternative electron acceptor for O2-limited zones (hence facultative). Confidence is 'medium' because Paulick 2009 itself is paywalled (Wiley 403); the recipe was reconstructed from four independent Thormann-lab papers that cite Paulick 2009 for LM medium and agree on the formulation (NaCl reported as 100 mM in Kreienbaum 2020 vs 200 mM in Rick 2022).
